title: CI-RED-016-C: calibrate legacy /exports consumer map (map correction + retain/defer disposition)
intent: Fix a namespace + reference-form blind spot in the CI-RED-016-B endpoint consumer-map scanner that made the legacy DXF /exports/* surface (app.routers.legacy_dxf_exports_router) read as consumer-less. The scanner recognized only the /api, /health, /ws, and /instrument URL namespaces and only matched path literals that immediately follow the opening quote; the legacy router is mounted at /exports (outside /api) and its frontend consumer calls it via a template base (`${API_BASE}/exports/...`). Add /exports as a recognized root and recognize the /exports template-base form, conservatively. Regenerate the audit artifacts and record a retain/defer disposition. No endpoint behavior, route count, or router baseline changes.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) build_endpoint_consumer_map.py: added ENDPOINT_ROOTS=(api,health,ws,instrument,exports) and rebuilt the endpoint-literal regex from it, so root-relative /exports literals are recognized; added a second, /exports-scoped regex (ENDPOINT_TEMPLATE_SUFFIX_RE) that captures the static suffix behind an interpolated base (`${API_BASE}/exports/polyline_dxf` -> /exports/polyline_dxf); extract_endpoint_literals now unions both matchers; JSON + Markdown methodology text updated to name /exports and describe the root-relative-vs-template-base coverage. The scanner also treats its own implementation file, unit test, and generated CI-RED-016 audit reports as self-observation paths for /exports evidence, and omits their unmatched literals to keep examples from self-registering as consumers. (2) test_endpoint_consumer_map_builder.py: added regression tests for /exports root-relative literal, /exports template-base normalization for polyline_dxf/biarc_dxf/dxf/health, query-string suffix handling, the real frontend curvemath_dxf.ts witness, the intentional non-match of /api template-base (scope proof), asset-path rejection, and self-observation filtering; existing /api and /health extraction tests unchanged. (3) Regenerated endpoint_consumer_map.json + CI_RED_016_ENDPOINT_CONSUMER_MAP.md. (4) Added CI_RED_016C_LEGACY_EXPORT_CLUSTER_DISPOSITION.md and a CI-RED-016-C note in SPRINTS.md (CI-RED-016 stays OPEN).
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: The scanner is an observation instrument that was miscalibrated: it had a namespace blind spot (/exports was not in the recognized roots) and a reference-form blind spot (template-base calls behind an interpolated base). Both produced false 'no_first_party_consumer_found' readings for six live endpoints, three of which have a real frontend consumer. This is not a cosmetic refactor: it changes what the audit artifact reports (three /exports routes move from no-consumer to frontend_product) and thereby changes which endpoints are eligible consolidation candidates. Isolating the scanner change from base drift confirms exactly three primary_consumer_class changes, all under /exports, zero outside; mounted/openapi stay 1132, static 1181, gap 49. The parallel /api template-base improvement is intentionally NOT taken here (it would reclassify ~13 unrelated /api endpoints) and is recorded as a follow-up so this PR stays scoped to the legacy export cluster.

commands_run: pytest tests/test_endpoint_consumer_map_builder.py -q -> 13 passed (6 pre-existing + 7 new /exports/self-observation regression tests)
python scripts/build_endpoint_consumer_map.py -> regenerated JSON+MD, exit 0, Mounted endpoint operations: 1132 / Static decorator count: 1181
isolate scanner effect (unmodified@ba3c8565 vs modified@ba3c8565): exactly 3 primary_consumer_class changes, all /exports -> frontend_product, 0 changes outside /exports

witness layer-two closure: POST /exports/polyline_dxf, POST /exports/biarc_dxf, GET /exports/dxf/health all show frontend_product evidence from packages/client/src/utils/curvemath_dxf.ts; the three /exports/history* routes remain no_first_party_consumer_found (true zero, no first-party literal or template caller)

self-observation check: services/api/scripts/build_endpoint_consumer_map.py, services/api/tests/test_endpoint_consumer_map_builder.py, and generated CI-RED-016 audit reports do not contribute /exports consumer evidence; their unmatched literals are omitted
namespace-coverage check: top-level segments of all 1132 mounted endpoints {api:1122, exports:6, instrument:2, health:1, ws:1} are all recognized by ENDPOINT_ROOTS post-fix; 0 unrecognized namespaces

route-surface counts unchanged: mounted_endpoint_count=1132, openapi_operation_count=1132, static total=1181, mounted_vs_static_gap=49; unmatched literal count drops 555 -> 545 after omitting self-observation unmatched literals

files.intent: Recognize the /exports namespace and its template-base call form so the legacy export surface is no longer invisible to the consumer scan.
files.behavior_change: The audit scanner now recognizes /exports root-relative literals (all roots) and /exports template-base suffixes (`${BASE}/exports/...`). It also prevents scanner/test/generated-report /exports examples from self-registering as consumer evidence. This changes generated audit classifications for the /exports cluster only; it does not touch any runtime endpoint. Template-base matching is deliberately scoped to /exports to avoid reclassifying unrelated /api endpoints in this PR.

files.intent: Lock in /exports root-relative + template-base extraction, query-string suffixes, the real frontend curvemath_dxf.ts witness, the intentional /api template-base non-match, asset rejection, and self-observation filtering.
Regenerated machine-readable map with corrected /exports consumer evidence (base ba3c8565).
files.behavior_change: Generated audit artifact: three /exports routes move no-consumer -> frontend_product; self-observation unmatched literals are omitted; base_commit + pre-existing base-drift lines (duplicate_route_count, route_object_entry_count) update from regenerating on current main. No route/router count change.

files.intent: Regenerated human-readable map + methodology notes naming the /exports namespace coverage.
Disposition packet: retain/defer the legacy /exports cluster with corrected consumer evidence and named follow-ups.
Record the CI-RED-016-C progress note; CI-RED-016 remains OPEN.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Map-calibration + disposition only. No runtime endpoint behavior, no route/router baseline change. The single medium-risk file (the scanner) changes only how the audit artifact classifies the /exports cluster; the change is isolated to exactly 3 /exports endpoints and verified to produce zero churn outside /exports. The broader /api template-base scanner improvement is deferred to a follow-up.
